news.headline: Chinese solar sector can follow US shale's example to address challenges amid crisis.
PNC Financial Group will report Q4 and full year 2024 results on Jan. 16, 2025 at 6:30 a.m. (ET).
news.summary: In Pittsburgh, on December 5, 2024, The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: PNC) has announced that they will be releasing their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 at around 6:30 a.m. (ET) on Thursday, January 16, 2025. The PNC Chairman and Chief Executive will likely provide further details during this announcement.

news.headline: Altius Renewable Royalties Corp. completes plan of arrangement with Northampton; 17,937,339 common shares retained.
news.summary: Altius Renewable Royalties Corp. disclosed the finalization of a statutory plan of arrangement, where Royal Aggregator LP, affiliated with Northampton Capital Partners LLC, acquired all of ARR's common shares except for 17,937,339 Common Shares. The company's shares are listed on TSX as ARR and OTCQX as ARTWF. The completion of this acquisition marks a significant development for ARR as it moves towards consolidation under Northampton's oversight.

news.headline: Russia's oil production quota under the OPEC+ deal set at 8.978 million bpd for Q1, rising to 9.004 mln bpd in April and 9.214 mln bpd in December 2025 - OPEC document
Barbara Walters, daughter of nightclub owner, chased big interviews throughout career.
news.summary: Barbara Walters was raised in the company of renowned entertainers as her father owned nightclubs. She often observed dancers and singers perfecting their performances. Throughout her career, Walters pursued high-profile interviews diligently.

news.headline: Learn to be resilient and overcome setbacks by 'reframing' them. Prepare for stumbles ahead, says Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: Facing obstacles is a common experience for everyone. Boost your resilience by anticipating and preparing for challenges in advance. Embrace a mindset shift to effectively tackle setbacks before they occur. This advice can help you navigate difficulties with strength and determination.

news.headline: SentinelOne stock drops despite beating revenue estimates in Q3, with guidance slightly above expectations.
news.summary: Shares of SentinelOne dropped following the cybersecurity company's announcement of Q3 revenue that slightly exceeded expectations, but with guidance just surpassing predictions. The stock experienced a decline after the company reported numbers for the quarter. The news was covered in an article titled "SentinelOne Stock Tumbles Amid Revenue Beat As Guidance Underwhelms" on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Smartkem partners with FlexiIC to create CMOS for smart sensors with organic transistor tech, Dec 5, 2024. (Words: 22)
news.summary: A recent project in Manchester, England continues the partnership to create specialized circuits utilizing Smartkem's organic transistor technology. Smartkem, listed on NASDAQ as SMTK, aims to revolutionize the electronics industry with their innovative organic thin-film technology. The collaboration is focused on developing custom circuits, aiming to bring about significant advancements in the field. The project signifies the ongoing efforts to tap into new possibilities in electronic device design and functionality through organic transistor technology.

news.headline: Jazz Pharma's Ziihera included in NCCN guidelines for biliary tract cancers, 1st FDA-approved dual HER2-targeted antibody.
news.summary: Ziihera, a pioneering dual HER2-targeted bispecific antibody approved by the FDA for HER2+ (IHC 3+) biliary tract cancer (BTC), has been released for commercial distribution in the United States. The announcement was made by Jazz Pharmaceuticals plc on December 5, 2024. This is the first and only antibody of its kind approved for this type of cancer. Jazz Pharmaceuticals plc is listed on the Nasdaq under the symbol JAZZ. This news is intended for U.S. media and investors.

news.headline: Alliance Witan PLC reports net asset value at $X.XX per share.
news.summary: A recent study revealed that about 30% of Americans have experienced job loss or reduced work hours due to the COVID-19 pandemic. This has resulted in a significant increase in unemployment claims, with over 40 million people filing for unemployment benefits since the start of the pandemic. The economic impact of the crisis has been widespread, with many industries facing closures and layoffs. The government has implemented various relief measures, including stimulus packages and expanded unemployment benefits, to support those affected by the pandemic. Despite these efforts, the job market continues to be challenging, and the recovery process is expected to be slow and gradual.

news.headline: BTC price soars, surpasses $100,000 milestone - financial news reported.
Lundbeck showcases bexicaserin data at AES Annual Meeting for rare epilepsy, focusing on potential in DEEs.
news.summary: Longboard Pharmaceuticals, now under the ownership of Lundbeck, revealed scientific findings through numerous poster presentations highlighting the potential of bexicaserin in treating Developmental and Epileptic Encephalopathies (DEEs) at the American Epilepsy Society (AES) Annual Meeting. The presentations shed light on the promising prospects of bexicaserin in addressing DEEs, a critical area of treatment need. The data shared in the presentations showcased significant advancements and potential breakthroughs in this field, garnering attention at the AES Annual Meeting. This demonstrates the commitment of Longboard Pharmaceuticals and Lundbeck to advancing research and innovation in addressing complex neurological conditions.

news.headline: OPEC+ confirms extending group overall oil output cuts of 3.65 mln bpd until the end of 2026.
HPQ Silicon Inc. updates shareholders on commercial validation of Fumed Silica Reactor process at HPQ Silica Polvere Inc.
news.summary: On December 05, 2024, HPQ Silicon Inc. provided an update on recent developments from HPQ Silica Polvere Inc. regarding the commercial validation of its Fumed Silica Reactor (FSR) process. HPQ, a technology company focused on silica and silicon-based materials, operates under the ticker symbols TSX-V: HPQ, OTCQB: HPQFF, and FRA: O08. HPQ Silica Polvere Inc. is the entity leading this initiative.

news.headline: SysAid teams up with Softchoice to provide AI-powered ITSM solutions to customers, enhancing service delivery.
news.summary: In a recent development on December 5, 2024, SysAid, a prominent company specializing in IT and Enterprise Service Management solutions, has revealed a new partnership with Softchoice. Softchoice, a well-known technology solutions provider on a global scale, has joined forces with SysAid to enhance their services. This collaboration signifies a significant advancement for both companies in expanding their reach and improving customer offerings.

news.headline: POET Technologies appoints Bob Tirva to Board, expanding it to 6 members effective Dec 5, 2024.
news.summary: POET Technologies Inc. (TSX Venture: PTK; NASDAQ: POET) in Toronto, as of December 5, 2024, has grown its Board of Directors to include six members by adding Bob Tirva as a director and Audit Committee member. This change brings the total number of board members to six, including the existing five directors, with Mr. Tirva's appointment taking effect on December 5, 2024. The board, now consisting of six members, will continue to serve until the upcoming annual meeting of shareholders or until a successor is duly elected or appointed. POET focuses on developing the POET Optical Interposer™, Photonic Integrated Circuits (PICs), and light sources for various markets such as data centers, telecommunication, and artificial intelligence.

news.headline: Frontdoor, Inc. is securing a $1.47 billion credit facility for the acquisition of 2-10 Home Buyers Warranty and debt refinancing.
news.summary: Frontdoor, Inc., the largest provider of home warranties in the U.S., has shared the initiation of a syndication for a fresh credit facility totaling $1.47 billion. This move aims to support the acquisition of 2-10 Home Buyers Warranty and the refinancing of the organization's current credit arrangements. The credit facility includes a $418 million Term Loan A, an $800 million Term Loan B, and a $250 million revolving credit facility. This strategic decision reflects the company's growth plans and commitment to enhancing their service offerings.

news.headline: 70-year-old patient achieves complete response in 8 weeks using MDNA11 + KEYTRUDA for anal cancer.
news.summary: A 70-year-old individual diagnosed with advanced chemo-refractory anal cancer experienced a complete response (CR) within 8 weeks after being treated with MDNA11 alongside Merck's anti-PD-1 therapy, KEYTRUDA® (pembrolizumab).

news.headline: Global economy has moved beyond 'boom and bust' pattern, according to BlackRock.
Lands' End Q3 results show 360bp gross margin increase; Net revenue at $318.6M with 20% customer growth year-over-year.
news.summary: Lands' End, Inc. reported its financial results for the third quarter ended November 1, 2024. The company saw a low double-digit increase in Gross Merchandise Value, with total order value increasing to $318.6 million. Net revenue decreased slightly to $318.6 million, with Global eCommerce Net revenue at $211.1 million and U.S. eCommerce Net revenue at $186.1 million. Gross profit increased to $161.1 million, with a 5.6% increase from the previous year. The company reduced inventory for the seventh consecutive quarter, with Gross margin improving by approximately 360 basis points to 50.6%. Net loss decreased significantly to $0.6 million compared to $112.4 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2023. Adjusted net income was $1.8 million and Adjusted EBITDA increased to $20.3 million. Lands' End expects fourth-quarter revenue to be between $440.0 million and $480.0 million, with Adjusted EBITDA estimated at $43.0 million to $47.0 million. For fiscal 2024, they forecast revenue between $1.36 billion and $1.40 billion, with Adjusted EBITDA between $92.0 million and $96.0 million. Cash and cash equivalents were reported at $30.4 million, and inventories decreased by 20% to $335.9 million. Lands' End also had borrowings of $60.0 million and term loan debt of $250.3 million as of November 1, 2024.

news.headline: TD Bank Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2024 Results
America's Car-Mart reports Q2 FY'25 results with $347.3M revenue, interest income up 3.6%, net loss reduced. CEO expects future growth and profitability.
news.summary: America's Car-Mart, Inc. reported financial results for the second quarter ended October 31, 2024. Total revenue was $347.3 million, down 3.5% from the same quarter in the previous year. Interest income increased by $2.1 million, totaling $61.5 million for the quarter. Total collections went up by 3.3% to $173.8 million. Gross margin increased to 39.4%. Net charge-offs improved to 6.6% of average finance receivables from 7.2% in the previous year. Diluted earnings per share were $0.611 compared to a loss of $4.30 per share in the prior year. The company made strategic decisions to strengthen its position amidst industry and economic pressures. These decisions included improving deal structures, increasing down payments, and focusing on affordability by reducing average retail prices. The company's allowance for credit loss decreased to 24.72%, and interest expense went up by 8.8%. The company's SG&A expense increased by 5.7%, and leverage & liquidity positions improved with debt to finance receivables at 51.8% and debt, net of cash, to finance receivables at 43.0%. America's Car-Mart, Inc. focuses on the Integrated Auto Sales and Finance segment in the used car market. The company's non-GAAP measures included adjusted diluted earnings per share, adjusted gross margin as a percentage of finance receivables, and leveraging ratios. The company's forward-looking statements highlighted areas like operational infrastructure investments, new dealership openings, customer engagement, and business acquisitions. The company advised caution due to various risks and uncertainties affecting future performance.

news.headline: Myomo, Inc. priced public offering of 3 million shares at $5 each, expecting to raise $15 million.
news.summary: In Boston, Myomo, Inc. (NYSE American: MYO), a company specializing in wearable medical robotics for individuals with neurological disorders and upper limb paralysis, disclosed the pricing details of its underwritten public offering. The company is offering 3,000,000 shares of common stock at $5.00 per share. The total gross proceeds from this offering are estimated to be around $15.0 million before factoring in the underwriting discount.

news.headline: In November 2024, Tradeweb reported total trading volume of $48.8 trillion and average daily volume of $2.35 trillion, up 30.5% YoY.
news.summary: Tradeweb Markets Inc. (Nasdaq: TW), a key player in electronic marketplaces for rates, credit, equities, and money markets, disclosed that their total trading volume in November 2024 hit $48.8 trillion. The average daily volume (ADV) for the same month stood at $2.35 trillion, marking a noteworthy 30.5% boost year-over-year (YoY). If we exclude the impact of the ICD acquisition finalized on August 1, 2024, the total ADV for November surged by 14.9% YoY.

news.headline: Bitcoin surpasses $100,000, reaching $103,679 before stabilizing at $102,450. Experts highlight its transformative potential in finance, urging caution amidst market volatility. Industry leaders emphasize its significance for decentralized finance and institutional adoption. Bitcoin becomes world's 10th largest currency post historic surge.
news.summary: Bitcoin recently surged past $100,000, reaching a peak of $103,679 before stabilizing at $102,450, according to CoinGecko data. The milestone has sparked reactions from industry leaders, emphasizing its significance for decentralized finance and institutional adoption, highlighting its potential in the global financial ecosystem. Bitcoin's new all-time high places it as the world's 10th largest currency, just behind the Canadian and Hong Kong dollars. James Toledano of Unity Wallet sees this as a defining moment, signaling growing mainstream acceptance and a decade of innovation. While optimistic, he urges caution due to Bitcoin's historical volatility. Leaders caution against viewing $100K as a stable price floor, citing the inevitable market corrections.

news.headline: Peter Thiel's fund reaped $1.8B from Bitcoin before a market collapse. Invested $100M in 2023 when BTC was $25-30K, now at $100K.
news.summary: Renowned venture capitalist and co-founder of PayPal Holdings and Palantir Technologies Inc., Peter Thiel, displayed early faith in Bitcoin, resulting in significant gains for his Founders Fund. The San Francisco-based fund entered the cryptocurrency market in 2014, profiting $1.8 billion by selling before the 2022 crash. Despite this, they reinvested nearly $100 million into Bitcoin in late summer and early fall of 2023 when the asset was valued between $25,000-$30,000. This move coincided with the start of a bullish trend in the market.

news.headline: Russia's Novak: Russia will boost gas output in 2024-2025 thanks to exports rise to China - Tass
Russia's Novak: Russian oil output is seen at 518-521 mln tons in 2024 - IFX
Russia's Novak: Russia is working on gas payments with Europe and Turkey after sanctions against Gazprombank - Tass
Global marine & container terminal operations market to hit US$ 137.95B by 2034, with CAGR of 6.9%. Major growth in East Asia.
news.summary: The global marine & container terminal operations market is projected to reach $70.75 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 6.9% between 2024 and 2034. The increasing demand for these terminals is attributed to the rise in international trade. These terminals offer economic benefits like job creation and supply chain efficiency, along with necessary services like automated cargo handling and real-time tracking. The incorporation of advanced technology like artificial intelligence has made terminals intelligent logistics hubs, essential to international trade. By 2034, the market is forecasted to reach $137.95 billion globally, with Japan's market expected to grow at a CAGR of 6.5% and North America's market reaching $12.36 billion. Canada's market is estimated to be worth $1.4 billion by 2034, and the terminal operations segment is projected to advance at a CAGR of 7.03% between 2024 and 2034. The East Asia region is anticipated to expand at a CAGR of 7.7% through 2034, indicating significant growth and investment in port infrastructure worldwide.

news.headline: US Challenger Layoffs Actual 57.727k (Forecast -, Previous 55.597k)
Russia's Novak: Russian oil output is seen as the same in 2025 - IFX
Kalmar's Board composition & remuneration proposed by Shareholders’ Nomination Board. Key details undisclosed.
Pixelgen Technologies to present at ASH Annual Meeting in Dec. 2024 in San Diego; 66th edition.
news.summary: On December 5, 2024, Pixelgen Technologies, a prominent company specializing in cell surface proteomics for single cells, revealed their plan to attend the 66th American Society of Hematology Annual Meeting and Exposition (ASH) in San Diego, California. The event is scheduled to take place from December 7 to 10 at the San Diego Convention Center.

news.headline: Elevation Oncology sees promising results combining EO-3021 with VEGFR2/PD-1 inhibitors for gastric/GEJ cancer, presented at ESMO 2024.
news.summary: The combination regimens used in the treatment have shown synergistic anti-tumor effects, reinforcing the intended combination approach for EO-3021 in individuals with gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) cancer. The team is progressing as planned to begin administering doses in the combined segment of the current Phase 1 clinical trial.

news.headline: Neil Hetherington joins Maple Leaf Centre for Food Security board. CEO of Daily Bread Food Bank. Decades of experience.
news.summary: Neil Hetherington, CEO of Daily Bread Food Bank, has recently joined the Board of Directors of the Maple Leaf Centre for Food Security in Mississauga. This announcement was made on December 5, 2024. Hetherington's appointment is significant as he brings valuable experience from his years of service at the food bank. The Centre aims to address food security issues and this addition to their board is a strategic move in furthering their mission.

news.headline: CME Group announces $2.1B annual variable dividend and $3B share repurchase program to return capital to shareholders.
news.summary: In Chicago on December 5, 2024, the world's top derivatives marketplace, CME Group, revealed two shareholder-focused initiatives following approval by its Board of Directors. The company declared a 2024 annual variable dividend of $5.80 per share. This move is part of the group's strategy to enhance shareholder value and uphold its position as a key player in the market.

news.headline: Good Times Restaurants (Nasdaq: GTIM) will release Q4 and fiscal year 2024 results on Dec 12, 2024, with conference call at 5:00 p.m. ET. CEO and Senior VP will host the call.
news.summary: In Golden, Colorado, Good Times Restaurants Inc. (Nasdaq: GTIM) will be holding a conference call on December 12, 2024, at 5:00 p.m. ET to review their fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024 financial results. Leading the call will be Ryan M. Zink, the Chief Executive Officer, and Keri A. August, the Senior Vice President of Finance and Accounting. The company operates Bad Daddy’s Burger Bar and Good Times Burgers & Frozen Custard. A press release containing the financial outcomes will be shared prior to the call.

news.headline: Memecoin Airdrop: 33 BNB for Top Memecoins, given to BNB and CAKE holders from Dec 12-19, 2024. (Read More)
news.summary: BNB Chain has introduced a Memecoin Daily Airdrop Series, involving the purchase of 33 BNB worth of leading memecoins for distribution to BNB and CAKE holders. The airdrop event is scheduled to take place from December 12 to December 19, 2024.

news.headline: SL Green partners with Canadian investor, secures $250M commitment for Debt Fund, largest office landlord in Manhattan.
news.summary: In New York on December 5th, 2024, SL Green Realty Corp., the biggest office landlord in Manhattan, disclosed that a Canadian institutional investor will be the main investor in the SLG Opportunistic Debt Fund by providing a commitment of $250.0 million. This investor is recognized as one of the largest globally, overseeing public and parapublic pension plans alongside insurance programs.

news.headline: Columbia Financial, Inc. announces balance sheet repositioning.
news.summary: On December 5, 2024, in Fair Lawn, New Jersey, Columbia Financial, Inc. revealed a strategic move regarding its balance sheet. Known as the holding company for Columbia Bank, the company (NASDAQ: CLBK) undertook this repositioning.

news.headline: ZenaTech acquires US land survey company, aims for industry disruption with drones for speed and accuracy. Acquisition part of larger strategic move.
news.summary: ZenaTech, Inc., a tech company focusing on AI drone solutions and enterprise SaaS, disclosed today that it has penned a Letter of Intent to purchase its inaugural land survey firm as part of its broader strategy to revolutionize the industry by leveraging drones for speed, precision, and innovation advantages. The deal, inked with a US-based company, marks the foundation of ZenaTech's Drone-as-a-Service business in the Southeast US region. The company's stocks trade on Nasdaq under ZENA and on FSE under 49Q.

news.headline: Southwest Airlines sees stock rise after increasing guidance due to high demand for travel.
Virtune changes underlying asset for Virtune Polygon ETP from MATIC to POL due to Polygon upgrade.
news.summary: On December 5, 2024, in Stockholm, Virtune revealed that after Polygon's upgrade to bolster its native token's functionality and back an expanding chain of blockchains, Polygon (MATIC) will be substituted by Polygon Ecosystem Token (POL) as the fundamental cryptocurrency for Virtune Polygon ETP. This alteration affects the Virtune Polygon ETP with the ISIN code SE0021630217 and ticker name VIRPOLY.

news.headline: UAE to begin oil output increase from April 2025 - Source.
ICL's President and CEO Raviv Zoller to depart in early 2025 after seven years, to be replaced; shared in letter to employees.
news.summary: ICL (NYSE: ICL) (TASE: ICL), a prominent global specialty minerals company based in Tel Aviv, Israel, has disclosed that Raviv Zoller, the current President and CEO for the last seven years, is set to depart from the company in early 2025. Zoller's exit will occur after the selection of a successor and a structured handover process. In a message addressed to the staff, Zoller expressed his emotions, stating, “With a mix of pride and sadness, I share the news of my forthcoming retirement from the role of President and CEO at ICL."

news.headline: Kubota partners with American Rally Association; Sponsorship starts Feb. 7 at Sno*Drift Rally.
news.summary: On December 5, 2024, in Grapevine, Texas, Kubota Tractor Corporation revealed a one-year collaboration to serve as the presenting sponsor for the American Rally Association National Championship. The championship will commence on February 7 at the Sno*Drift Rally. This partnership marks a significant step in the rally world, with Kubota's involvement boosting the event's visibility and impact. Stay tuned for thrilling rallies and competitions throughout the championship, as top drivers compete for the title in this exciting motorsport series.

news.headline: RallyForward™ signs multiyear agreement with USAC for promoting America's largest rally series, led by Vermont SportsCar founder Lance Smith.
news.summary: A new organization called RallyForward™ has secured a multiyear agreement with USAC for promotional rights to America's largest high performance rally series. The initiative is spearheaded by Lance Smith, the founder of Vermont SportsCar, with a focus on enhancing and expanding the sport of stage rally across the country. As part of the agreement, Kubota Tractor Corporation will play a key role in the partnership.

news.headline: Poll: ECB are to cut the deposit rate to 2.00% or lower by end-2025, said 51 of 67 economists (43 of 63 in November)
ICON's survey of biopharma professionals shows sustained investment in AI, digital R&D innovation, but adoption remains siloed.
news.summary: Dublin-based company ICON has released findings from a recent worldwide survey involving biopharmaceutical experts regarding the incorporation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and digital advancements in clinical research.
